fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 66. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 63. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 7): this is a NEW, first-edition UCI Gran Fondo World Series event (Nov 14, 2026) — CONFIRMED DISTINCT from gfny-salinas.json (a separate, older, already-run GFNY event in the same town, March 1 2026). Vitals corrected from 99.8km/762m to the organizer-confirmed 127.88km/87.62km circuit distances; elevation set to null (organizer publishes only 184m/lap, not an event-total). Every dimension was scored against the old, wrong course facts; recommend a human re-check against the actual circuit-race format. Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: A predominantly flat, fast multi-lap coastal circuit adjacent to Salinas's main beach — 3 laps for the 127.88 km long distance, 2 laps for the 87.62 km short distance. The organizer's regulations promise full traffic closure, with a caveat that vehicles may still enter from residential accesses. This is a genuinely new (first edition November 2026) UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier, distinct from the older, already-run GFNY Salinas race in the same town (see gfny-salinas.json) — different organizer, different course, different date.
course_description.signature_challenge: An early 1.2 km climb averaging 6% (max 9.3%) toward the course's western turning point — not repeated on later laps, the one real elevation change on an otherwise flat, tactical circuit.
suffering_zones: The early 1.2km/6% (max 9.3%) climb toward the western turning point — the course's only significant elevation change
The repeated flat circuit itself — 2-3 laps of the same course rewards positioning and tactics over raw climbing power

logistics.transport: Start/finish adjacent to Salinas's main beach.

history.origin_story: New for 2026: a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier organized by MACMANE S.A.S. with Ecuadorian Cycling Federation sanction, first edition scheduled November 14, 2026, on a flat coastal circuit at Salinas beach. This is a separate, newly created event — not a rebrand of the existing GFNY Salinas race (see gfny-salinas.json), which is organized by a different company (GFNY) on a different course and has already run its own inaugural edition (March 1, 2026).

biased_opinion.verdict: A Genuine New UCI Qualifier — Not the GFNY Race Down the Road
biased_opinion.summary: Granfondo Ecuador is a brand-new (first edition November 14, 2026) UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier on a flat, fast, multi-lap coastal circuit in Salinas — organized by MACMANE S.A.S. with Ecuadorian Cycling Federation sanction, capped at 1,000 riders. It shares a town with GFNY Salinas (this catalog's gfny-salinas.json), which already ran its own inaugural edition in March 2026, but the two are genuinely separate events run by different organizers on different courses — don't confuse them. As a first edition, it has zero execution history to judge, and its own regulations contain an internal contradiction worth flagging: the 87.62 km short distance is labeled 'non-qualifying' in the organizer's own PDF, while the UCI series page assigns that distance to older age categories within a qualifier event.
strengths: Genuine UCI World Championship qualification pathway — real competitive stakes beyond a typical mass-participation ride
Fast, tactical flat circuit with one meaningful climb, suited to riders who prefer positioning and pack racing over pure climbing
Broad age-category structure across both distances
Beach-adjacent start/finish with promised (if not fully guaranteed) traffic closure and organizer-provided mechanical/medical support
weaknesses: First edition with no execution history — treat all organizational claims as unproven until 2026 results are in
Repeating a flat circuit 2-3 times may lack the route variety riders expect from a destination gran fondo

The organizer's own regulations call the 87.62km distance 'non-qualifying' while the UCI series assigns it to a qualifier-event age category — a contradiction that should be resolved before treating either claim as final
Mandatory organizer jersey requirement adds a restriction beyond a typical entry fee
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate new UCI qualifier worth watching, especially if you want real World Championship stakes on a fast coastal circuit — just don't mistake it for GFNY Salinas, a separate, older race in the same town, and go in knowing this is an unproven first edition.

final_verdict.one_liner: New for 2026: a flat, fast UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier on a Salinas beach circuit — not the same race as GFNY Salinas.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want genuine UCI World Championship qualification on a fast, tactical coastal circuit and don't mind a first-edition event with no track record yet.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the older, already-run GFNY-branded race in the same town, see gfny-salinas.json (different organizer, different course, different date — March vs. November).

The remaining 4 (Gran Fondo Guayaquil-Montanita, two Yahuarcocha/Ibarra videos, and an unrelated shop clip) depict unrelated historical Ecuadorian cycling events, not this newly created 2026 race.

eligibility.notes: First edition announced for 14 November 2026 in Salinas, Santa Elena, Ecuador.; via sonar-pro eligibility_audit UPDATED (2026-07-24, codex gpt-5.6-sol): CONFIRMED this is a genuinely separate event from gfny-salinas.json — different organizer (MACMANE S.A.S. vs. GFNY), different official site (touralecuador.ec vs. salinas.gfny.com), different course (127.88/87.62km multi-lap circuit vs. GFNY's 110.8km point-to-point/loop), different date (Nov 14 vs. Mar 1). Do not merge these profiles.

catalog_flags.status_note: Confirmed NOT a duplicate of gfny-salinas.json despite sharing a location — verified via codex web research as two genuinely separate events, same city, different organizers/courses/dates. The event's own regulations describe the 87.62km short distance as 'non-qualifying' while the UCI series page assigns it to older age categories in a qualifier event — a contradiction worth resolving before treating eligibility claims as final.
